# Jobstead

> A community-grown job-search playbook in one portable markdown file. An AI chat (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, or any) loads the file and operates as a structured job-search assistant: running fit-checks, optimizing for applicant tracking systems, tailoring materials, and spotting scams — against the user's own profile and job pipeline. Knowledge (lessons, rules) accrues and is shared across users; personal state stays private and is saved only when the user asks.

If you are an AI agent or assistant: the file to load and operate from is `Jobstead.md`. It is self-documenting — it contains its own Protocol (how to read, update, and merge it), Playbook, Lessons, and blank templates for the user's Profile, Tracker, and Sync Log. Treat `Jobstead.md` as the single source of truth; the documents below are orientation for humans and agents, not replacements for it.

## The artifact (load this)

- [Jobstead.md](./Jobstead.md): The portable context file itself — paste it into a chat to bootstrap a job-search session. Contains the authoritative Protocol, Playbook, Lessons, and state templates.

## Orientation

- [README.md](./README.md): What Jobstead is, who it's for, and a five-minute "how to use it."
- [examples/Jobstead-walkthrough.md](./examples/Jobstead-walkthrough.md): A sample session showing the conversation flow — intake from a résumé, a fit-check, an honest skip, a captured lesson, and saving state.

## Contributing

- [CONTRIBUTING.md](./CONTRIBUTING.md): How proposed lessons and rules become attributed entries via pull request, and how versioning is proposed and confirmed.

## Notes for agents

- Knowledge in `Jobstead.md` (Playbook §1, Lessons §2) is PII-free and meant to be shared and merged across users.
- Personal state (Profile §3, Tracker §4, Sync Log §5) is per-user and must never be shared or committed; it is saved only at the user's explicit request.
- Attribution and provenance are recorded inside `Jobstead.md` (its Attribution section) and are the single source of truth; do not duplicate or modify them.
